note: Connectors the decomposition prompt may reference. type: leading = early-warning signal; confirming = hard validation. The prompt may ONLY use source_ids that appear here. 'serves' lists the claim archetypes each source is good for.

sources.name: Polymarket
sources.notes: Implied probability of an event. Semantic-match break conditions to live markets — often a market IS the break condition.

sources.name: Kalshi
sources.notes: CFTC-regulated. US econ prints: CPI, Fed decisions, jobs.

sources.name: Metaculus
sources.notes: Calibrated community forecasts. Best for tech/science milestones and timelines.

sources.name: Manifold Markets
sources.notes: Play-money but very broad long-tail coverage of niche company/tech questions.

sources.name: Google Trends
sources.notes: Search interest as a consumer-demand proxy (pytrends).

sources.name: Wikipedia Pageviews
sources.notes: Attention proxy; clean official API.

sources.name: Hacker News (Algolia)
sources.notes: Developer/tech adoption + sentiment.

sources.name: Reddit (targeted subs)
sources.notes: High signal but API now paid/rate-limited. Use as a Watch trigger only — never the sole basis for a Broken state.

sources.name: StockTwits
sources.notes: Ticker-level retail sentiment; noisy.

sources.name: GitHub
sources.notes: Ecosystem-adoption proxy, e.g. CUDA vs ROCm vs Triton activity.

sources.name: Job Postings
sources.notes: Hiring/layoffs as expansion/contraction and strategic-intent signal.

sources.name: Web Traffic (Similarweb / Cloudflare Radar)
sources.notes: Product-usage growth proxy.

sources.name: App Store Rankings / Downloads
sources.notes: Consumer app demand (e.g. Sensor Tower).

sources.name: On-chain Labeled Wallets (Nansen / Arkham)
sources.notes: Labeled wallets with track record — not generic whale noise.

sources.name: Exchange In/Outflows
sources.notes: Inflows -> sell pressure; outflows -> accumulation.

sources.name: Unusual Options Activity
sources.notes: Volume/OI anomalies as smart-money positioning.

sources.name: Short Interest (FINRA)
sources.notes: Lagged but leading-ish for squeeze/sentiment.

sources.name: Commodity Spot/Term Price (LME / EIA / spot feeds)
sources.notes: Direct commodity price (uranium, oil, metals). Added in v1 after eval gap.

sources.name: SEC EDGAR (10-K/Q, 8-K, Form 4)
sources.notes: Hard numbers, insider trades, material events.

sources.name: Earnings Reports & Transcripts
sources.notes: Guidance, capex, margin/ASP commentary, KPIs.

sources.name: FRED (Macro Series)
sources.notes: Official macro series.

sources.name: Price & Volume (Polygon / Finnhub)
sources.notes: Confirms market reaction. Never a claim by itself.

sources.name: News (Finnhub / NewsAPI / GDELT)
sources.notes: Event confirmation; coincident, not leading.

sources.name: Sector Official (EIA / USDA / Baker Hughes / WNA)
sources.notes: Commodity fundamentals: energy, ag, rig counts, uranium.
